game_info.title: Neon Glitch: Gravity Runner
game_info.description:
**Neon Glitch: Gravity Runner** is a fast-paced precision platformer set inside a corrupted cybernetic mainframe. You play as a rogue data packet attempting to escape the system's deletion protocols.

Your primary mechanic is the **Gravity Drive**, which allows you to instantaneously flip your gravitational pull, letting you run on ceilings to avoid floor hazards, and vice versa. Navigate through treacherous security sectors filled with "Firewall" spikes, patrolling "Antivirus" drones, and rotating data-saws.

**Objective:**
- **Survive:** Navigate through 3 increasingly difficult sectors without being deleted.
- **Collect:** Gather yellow "Data Bits" to increase your score and restore system integrity.
- **Escape:** Reach the blue "Data Port" at the end of each level to advance.

Watch out for your health integrity—taking damage reduces it, and falling into the void or hitting a "Kill Zone" results in immediate deletion!
game_info.controls:
- **ENTER**: Start Game / Next Level
- **ESC**: Pause / Unpause
- **R**: Restart Game (Return to Title)
- **Arrow Left / Right**: Move character
- **Space**: Jump (works on floor and ceiling)
- **Z**: Invert Gravity (Flip between floor and ceiling)
- **Shift**: Dash (Short burst of speed and temporary immunity)

TEST_1.test_description: Verify basic movement physics, gravity inversion mechanics, and collision stability.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The automated controller will constantly move the player to the right. It will periodically trigger the Jump (Space) and Gravity Flip (Z) actions at random intervals to ensure the player transitions correctly between floor and ceiling states without clipping through geometry.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should move horizontally, successfully flip gravity, and land on surfaces (floor or ceiling) without getting stuck or falling out of bounds. The game should remain in the PLAYING state for at least 10 seconds.

TEST_2.test_description: Verify level completion capability and object interaction (collectibles/portals).
TEST_2.strategy_description: The controller will implement a "Greedy Seeker" behavior. It will move towards the X-coordinate of the exit portal. If a hazard is detected in a short raycast ahead, it will attempt to Jump. If the hazard is tall, it will Flip Gravity. It will continually adjust to move toward the goal.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player should navigate the terrain, collect at least one Data Bit, and eventually reach the Data Port to trigger the "Level Complete" or "Win" state. The test succeeds if the player reaches the portal or survives for a prolonged duration navigating obstacles.
